---
title: "Home Value by Neighbourhood"
description: "Median dwelling value across Canada's cities, by census tract — 2021 Census"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import os
import json
import pandas as pd
from pipeline.charts import choropleth_map
```

Median dwelling value for each **census tract** — roughly a neighbourhood of 2,500–8,000
people — from the 2021 Census. **Zoom and pan to your own area.** Census tracts cover cities
and larger towns; sparsely populated rural areas aren't divided into tracts, so they appear
blank.

This is the census's **owner-estimated** value (what owners think their home would sell for),
not a record of sale prices — open sale-price data by neighbourhood isn't available. It is the
same measure as the city-level [home-value map](index.qmd), at finer resolution.

This detailed view lives on its own page (≈3 MB of boundary data) so the main
[Housing & Cost of Living](index.qmd) page stays fast to load.

[← Back to Housing & Cost of Living](index.qmd)

```{python}
#| echo: false
geo_path = "data/geo/ct_2021.geojson"
dv_path = "data/geo/statcan_ct_dwelling_value_2021.csv"
if os.path.exists(geo_path) and os.path.exists(dv_path):
    ct_geo = json.load(open(geo_path))
    ct_df = pd.read_csv(dv_path, dtype={"ctuid": str}).dropna(subset=["dwelling_value"])
    zlo = float(ct_df["dwelling_value"].quantile(0.05))
    zhi = float(ct_df["dwelling_value"].quantile(0.95))
    fig = choropleth_map(ct_geo, ct_df, "ctuid", "dwelling_value", name_col="name",
        colorbar_title="Median dwelling value", value_prefix="$", colorscale="Viridis",
        zmin=zlo, zmax=zhi, height=720,
        source_note="Source: 2021 Census (Statistics Canada, Census Profile 98-401-X2021007); owner-estimated median dwelling value by census tract.")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "scrollZoom": True, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Census-tract home-value map not available — run `python -m pipeline.build_census_geo`.")
```

[← Back to Housing & Cost of Living](index.qmd)
